name: Cloud Kitchen
description: Grovyn Autopilot — Cloud Kitchen Operations Intelligence platform (multi-tenant ready).

services.name: backend
services.description: Core Data Service — REST API (Express, in-memory seeded data).

environment.description: Port the API server listens on.
Comma-separated list of allowed origins for the frontend(s) of this client.
HMAC secret for signing stateless session tokens. Must be unique per client/environment.
Password for the demo login flow. Override or disable per client via AUTH_DEMO_ENABLED.
Set to false to disable the built-in demo password login for a client's production deployment.
Identifier reported by the health endpoint; set per client for observability.

services.name: frontend
services.description: React + Vite dashboard, served as a static site (nginx) with runtime-injected branding config.

environment.description: Origin of the client's backend API (no trailing slash), e.g. https://api.<client>.grovyn.in
Product/company name shown throughout the UI.
Short form used in compact UI copy.
URL to the client's logo image.
Primary theme color as an HSL triplet (H S% L%), matches CSS custom property format.
Secondary/accent theme color as an HSL triplet.
Support contact email shown in the UI.
Support contact phone shown in the UI.
Company/restaurant address shown in the UI.
Show the demo login hint on the sign-in screen. Set to false for real client deployments.

notes: This file is consumed by the Grovyn deployment platform to build and run each service in this repository.

Frontend branding/runtime environment variables are also readable at container runtime via public/runtime-config.js (see deployment/README.md) so the same built image can serve multiple clients without a rebuild.
Additional client-specific values (branding, secrets, domains) are supplied entirely through environment variables — no source changes are required to onboard a new client.
